sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
To ensure kidney health and rule out potential complications.
History of Present Illness
Katherine is a 22-month-old former 34 and 3-week-old, twin with Smith-Magenis syndrome. She was born with a low birth weight, approximately 3 pounds, 13 ounces, and her twin was smaller, around 3 pounds, 8 ounces. Initially, she experienced difficulties with feeding and temperature regulation, requiring preemie-level care. She has a history of reflex and has been diagnosed with a syndrome. Currently, she is not walking or sitting independently, and she reports difficulty breathing. She was previously consuming only milk, but now takes ripple pea protein milk and water. She has been seen by a variety of specialists, including an orthopedic surgeon, ophthalmologist, audiology specialist, and a geneticist. She is currently receiving speech therapy, occupational therapy, and physical therapy. The patient's family lives in Dallas, Georgia, and there is no family history of kidney failure or dialysis.
Findings
Physical exam reveals a well-nourished female, slightly fussy upon examination. Her eyes appear small, and she exhibits mild hypotonia of the lower extremities. External genitalia are normal. The nurse noted frequent urination and excessive thirst.
Assessment

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Katherine presents with Smith-Magenis syndrome, potentially complicated by congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract. She demonstrates several developmental delays, including difficulty walking and sitting, and reports difficulty breathing. The potential for kidney fusion is a consideration based on her genetic diagnosis.
Plan
We will obtain a kidney ultrasound today to assess her renal function and rule out any abnormalities. The family will be scheduled for follow-up at the town center location if the ultrasound reveals any concerning findings. Speech therapy will be started within the next couple of weeks. The nurse will provide instructions for the ultrasound. We will follow up in four weeks if symptoms persist.
medical_concepts: congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract
congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract
